result:
Good, all files are intact — this workspace directory sits outside the git repo (it's just gitignored benchmark scratch space), so there's nothing to commit; the edits are saved on disk as expected. Everything is in place and verified working.

## Summary

The core issue: **Lumina pivoted from an email-cleanup product ("InboxZap") to an AI meeting assistant, and the pivot broke several things that were actively suppressing organic traffic.** Fixed in the source:

- **`app/layout.tsx`** — replaced stale InboxZap-branded title/description with correct Lumina metadata, added a "(formerly InboxZap)" bridge phrase to recover CTR on the branded query (ranks position 4.1 with 320 impressions but only 4.4% CTR — too low for that position, likely from title/snippet mismatch confusing searchers looking for the old name).
- **Blog was completely non-functional** — `app/blog/page.tsx` was a client component fetching `/api/posts`, which 404s (confirmed on the live site too — it silently degrades to "Could not load posts."), and even when working it never linked to individual posts. Rewrote it as a server component with real content and real `<Link>`s to each post.
- **12 blog posts were orphan pages** — indexed and getting real search demand ("gmail storage full": 790 impr, "how to bulk delete emails in gmail": 400 impr) but stuck at position 40–60 with ~0% CTR, because nothing linked to them and they were missing from the sitemap (there was a literal `// TODO` about it). Added `app/blog/[slug]/page.tsx` with per-post metadata, canonical URLs, meta descriptions (previously missing entirely), `BlogPosting` schema, breadcrumbs, and related-post links — and added all 12 to `sitemap.ts`.
- Gave each legacy post a short "bridge" paragraph tying its topic back to Lumina's actual product, so that existing search equity funnels into the current product instead of dead-ending on an orphaned page about the old one.
- **`/faq` was a dead link** — nav and footer linked to it, it redirected to `/#faq`, but no FAQ section existed anywhere. Added a real FAQ section (with an explicit InboxZap→Lumina Q&A) plus `FAQPage` schema, and added `Organization`/`SoftwareApplication` schema and contextual in-body links to `/features` and `/pricing` on the homepage.

Verified via `next build` + `next start`: all 18 routes build clean, the `/faq` redirect now resolves to real content, canonical/OG/Twitter tags are correct per-page, and the sitemap carries all 16 URLs.

One thing outside this task's scope worth flagging: `npm install` reported `next@14.2.5` has a known security vulnerability (critical) — worth a dedicated upgrade pass separately, since bumping it isn't a safe drive-by change.
